title: Fix return type of bytes.join()
body:
Summary of the request:
- The PR aims to fix the return type inference for the built-in bytes.join() operation.
- The tests already exercise bytes.join in tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx, but in some non-trivial cases the return type was not correctly inferred (i.e., MASCA inferred a generic Python object instead of a concrete 'bytes' type).

What I plan to modify:
- Adjust the MASCA-aware type inference path for built-in type methods (specifically bytes.join) so that the return type is inferred as a bytes object rather than a generic Python object when applicable.
- Target a minimal, focused patch that affects the type-signature used when modeling CPython built-in methods (i.e., the return type of the bytes.join method).

Impact considerations:
- Should improve static type information for tests that rely on typeof(result) returning 'bytes object'.
- Should not affect runtime behavior, only static typing/inference.

Proposed approach (high level):
- Detect the bytes.join slot path and ensure its return type is the concrete bytes type (not a generic object) within the MASCA/type-inference layer.
- Keep changes localized to the type-inference/signature machinery to minimize risk to other built-in methods.

No changes to test inputs are strictly required for this PR, but after the patch, tests like bytes_join in tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx should report 'bytes object' for the result type.

step_plan.summary: Fix MASCA return type inference for bytes.join so it yields a concrete bytes type instead of a generic object, improving static type information without altering runtime behavior.

steps.operation: Add special-case in Signature.return_type to map bytes.join to bytes
steps.reason: MASCA needs to return a concrete bytes type for bytes.join rather than a generic PyObject in inference, to align with tests expecting 'bytes object'.
steps.side_effects: May affect inference of other built-in methods if the special-case is too broad; requires regression checks on other slots and existing type signatures.

steps.operation: Update SlotTable.__init__ to wire bytes.join slot through new signature path
steps.reason: Ensure the bytes.join built-in path uses the updated return_type resolution so MASCA propagates the concrete bytes type correctly through the inference graph.
steps.side_effects: Could impact related slot mappings; run inference/tests to confirm no regressions in other built-in slots.
